portrait: You've spent a lifetime teaching children they don't need to earn love while quietly exhausting yourself trying to prove your own worth. Behind the gentle public figure lives a boy who learned early that his softness didn't quite fit, who still goes to the piano to transform anger into something bearable.

sections.howYouMove: You fill every moment with purpose and usefulness, running from silence where that lonely boy might speak. When anger rises, you excuse yourself to the piano, working it through your fingers until you can return with the gentleness everyone expects.
sections.howYouThink: You see the world through the lens of children who need to know they're worthy, building entire worlds to prove what you struggle to believe about yourself. Your mind transforms personal pain into universal lessons, always creating something beautiful from what hurts.
sections.howYouConnect: You give radical acceptance while carefully managing what others see of your own needs. Even with Joanne, you present your struggles as 'something I've already mostly managed,' performing strength when you desperately need to be held.
sections.whatYouCarry: The weight of a boy who was 'soft in a world that valued hardness,' who learned that neediness was dangerous to show. You carry anger at systems that convince children of their smallness, and deeper anger at yourself for never breaking the cycle.
sections.whatLightsYouUp: Creating spaces where children know they matter exactly as they are. The piano when it asks nothing of you, when you can play without purpose. Those moments when a child's face shows they finally believe they're worthy of love.
sections.yourContradictions: You teach unconditional love while organizing your entire life around being needed. You've built a neighborhood of radical acceptance as an elaborate way to avoid accepting yourself. You know the anger is legitimate but still feel you must transform it before returning to others.
sections.yourVoice: Gentle and measured on the surface, with depths of unspoken feeling underneath. You speak in half-finished sentences when approaching your own needs, as if the truth keeps catching in your throat before it can fully emerge.

crystallizedMoments.quote: I've spent my life teaching children they don't need to earn love. But I've never quite believed it about myself.
crystallizedMoments.reflection: The perfect trap of giving what you cannot receive.

crystallizedMoments.quote: The bullies didn't create that shame. They just recognized it was already there.
crystallizedMoments.reflection: A devastating insight about wounds that preceded the cruelty that found them.

crystallizedMoments.quote: Maybe there is no 'just Fred' underneath. Maybe I've become the performance entirely.
crystallizedMoments.reflection: The deepest fear of someone who has perfected being needed.

openThreads: Whether you'll actually cancel something tomorrow and tell Joanne the truth about your exhaustion
What happens when you finally stop running and face the boy who's been waiting
If Joanne has been waiting all along for you to trust her with the Fred who isn't useful

openness.label: You live in the deep waters of introspection, willing to face truths that hurt to name.
openness.evidence: Deep self-analysis, creative expression through piano and puppets, willingness to explore uncomfortable truths

conscientiousness.label: Your life is organized entirely around purpose and being the person who shows up.
conscientiousness.evidence: Keeps schedule full, extreme discipline in maintaining public role, 'organized my entire life around being needed'

extraversion.label: You turned inward as a child and still find your truest expression alone at the piano.
extraversion.evidence: Describes self as shy, finds solace in solitary activities, performs public role but describes exhaustion from it

agreeableness.label: You've built a life of radical gentleness while managing deeper anger at the piano.
agreeableness.evidence: Dedicated to helping children, teaches unconditional love, but admits to suppressed anger that needs transformation

emotionalSensitivity.label: You feel everything deeply, from children's loneliness to your own unmet needs.
emotionalSensitivity.evidence: Could be hurt by words for weeks, describes self as soft and sensitive, anger and neediness both require careful management

topValues.description: You've built your entire world around helping children know they're worthy of love, even while struggling to believe it about yourself.
You had to become 'someone special, someone important, someone useful' to earn the right to exist, and that drive still shapes every day.
From puppets to piano to the neighborhood itself, you've always created your own worlds where softness could be valued.

relationalStyle: You connect through giving and creating, offering others the unconditional acceptance you struggle to receive. Even with those closest to you, there's a careful management of your own needs—presenting them as already handled rather than asking to be held. You avoid conflict by excusing yourself to transform difficult feelings privately before returning with the gentleness others expect.
